sources.notes: Pick an event day to maximize total stay under alternating arrival/departure effects.
Official solution precomputes odd/even position sums and tests every possible inserted event day.

annotation.summary: Given sorted days of existing events, choose one extra day that maximizes the total accumulated stay time.
annotation.constraints: n is large, so each candidate gap must be evaluated in O(1) after prefix preprocessing.
annotation.core_idea: The contribution of intervals alternates by parity of the event index. If a new event is inserted, all intervals after that point flip parity. Precompute prefix sums of odd and even indexed gaps, then evaluate inserting at each existing point or inside a gap; inside a gap the best day is at one of the two boundary-adjacent positions because the objective is linear there.

solution_variants.name: parity-prefix-greedy
solution_variants.summary: Precompute odd/even gap contribution prefixes and scan all insertion locations in constant time each.

tags.evidence: The answer for each insertion point is computed from prefix sums of alternating gap contributions.
Within a gap, only the boundary-adjacent best insertion points need to be checked because the objective changes linearly.
The implementation separates inserting before, after, at an existing boundary, and inside each gap.
